name: Cheese Buns (Bulemas "Falsos" for Shelly)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate all cheeses together (you may use Parmesan, Romano cheese, or a mixture of both. Amounts are approximate).
2. Place some of the Parmesan/Romano cheese in a small bowl.
3. Mix the remaining grated cheese with the rest of the ingredients and make the dough using the "dough" cycle of the Breadmaker.
4. When done mixing, remove the dough from the pan and gently squeeze out any gas bubbles.
5. Make 12 balls of dough and place them temporarily in a baking pan lined with vegetable oil.
6. One at a time, take each ball and roll it into a six or seven inch circle using a rolling pin.
7. Place a generous strip of cheese mixture along one side of the rolled dough and roll up tightly to form a log.
8. Gently shape each log into a coil.
9. If needed, seal the end with a small amount of water.
10. Dip the tops of the buns in additional Parmesan on an ungreased cookie sheet.
11. Bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes or until lightly browned.
This recipe was adapted from Mom's recipe for Spinach Bulemas, but spinach is omitted as Shelly dislikes it. As part of our Sephardic (Jewish) family tradition, Bulemas and hard-boiled eggs are served before the main meal on holidays and after returning from synagogue on Saturdays.
